delivery_methods.code/tdd: Python code in scripts/pes/. Delivered via /nw:execute with nw-software-crafter. TDD with pytest.
delivery_methods.forge/command: New command markdown file. Delivered via /nw:forge.
delivery_methods.forge/skill: New skill markdown file. Delivered via /nw:forge or direct write.
delivery_methods.agent/edit: Modify existing agent markdown behavioral spec. Direct editing.
delivery_methods.command/edit: Modify existing command markdown. Direct editing.
delivery_methods.skill/edit: Modify existing skill markdown. Direct editing.
delivery_methods.batch/agent-edit: Identical pattern edit across multiple agent markdown files. Direct editing.

rejected_simple_alternatives.alternative: Configuration-only: environment variable for active proposal
rejected_simple_alternatives.expected_impact: 30% -- hooks could resolve paths, but agents and commands would still need filesystem detection
rejected_simple_alternatives.why_insufficient: Environment variables are session-scoped. Different terminal sessions could disagree on active proposal. File-based pointer persists across sessions.

rejected_simple_alternatives.expected_impact: 40% -- PES hooks would resolve correctly, but agent dispatch, dashboard, and switch command still missing
rejected_simple_alternatives.why_insufficient: Path resolution in hooks alone does not deliver multi-proposal UX. Dashboard enumeration, switch command, and agent path updates are required for usability.

Agents use Claude's tools (Glob, Read) to enumerate proposals. They never call Python directly. PES hooks only fire on Claude Code events, not on agent read operations. Enumeration logic belongs in the agent's behavioral spec and supporting skill.

phases.title: Foundation: Path Resolution for PES Hooks

steps.title: Path resolution module for PES hooks
steps.description: New Python module in scripts/pes/adapters/ that detects workspace layout (multi/legacy/fresh), reads active-proposal pointer, derives state_dir and artifact_base paths. Legacy fallback returns root paths. Used exclusively by hook_adapter.main().
acceptance_criteria: Multi-proposal layout detected when .sbir/proposals/ exists
Legacy layout detected when .sbir/proposal-state.json at root without proposals/
Fresh workspace detected when neither exists
Missing active-proposal file in multi-proposal workspace returns error with available proposal list
Legacy mode returns root paths identical to current behavior
architectural_constraints: Adapter layer only -- no domain imports
Pure function: CWD input, path strings output
Consumers: hook_adapter.main() only (agents do their own resolution via Read tool)

steps.title: Hook adapter integration with path resolution
steps.description: Replace hardcoded .sbir path in hook_adapter.main() (line 200) with path resolution module call. All hook events use resolved state_dir.
acceptance_criteria: Hook adapter resolves state_dir via path resolution module
PES enforcement scoped to active proposal state in multi-proposal workspace
Legacy workspaces produce identical hook behavior to current code
Audit log written to per-proposal audit directory
architectural_constraints: hook_adapter.main() is the only modification point
audit_dir derived from resolved state_dir

steps.title: Per-proposal namespace creation behavior in orchestrator
steps.description: Update sbir-orchestrator agent behavioral spec to create .sbir/proposals/{topic-id}/ and artifacts/{topic-id}/ on new proposal. Set active-proposal pointer. Detect and reject namespace collisions. Update proposal-new command docs.
acceptance_criteria: New proposal creates namespaced directories under .sbir/proposals/ and artifacts/
Active-proposal pointer updated to new proposal
Existing proposal state files unchanged after new proposal creation
Namespace collision produces error with --name suggestion
Fresh workspace uses multi-proposal layout from first proposal
architectural_constraints: Topic ID lowercased for filesystem safety
Orchestrator uses Bash/Write tools to create directories and files
No Python code needed -- this is agent filesystem operations

steps.title: Legacy workspace detection and opt-in migration
steps.description: Add legacy detection behavior to orchestrator agent. When /proposal new runs in legacy workspace, prompt for migration. Migration logic: agent-driven file copy/rename via Bash tool (or Python module if atomicity requires it).
steps.delivery: agent/edit (+ code/tdd if Python migration module needed)
acceptance_criteria: Legacy workspace detected and migration prompt shown on /proposal new
Migration moves state to .sbir/proposals/{topic-id}/
Migration moves artifacts to artifacts/{topic-id}/
Original files preserved as .migrated suffix
Legacy workspaces work unchanged without migration
architectural_constraints: Migration reads topic.id from existing state to derive namespace
Copy-then-rename pattern to prevent data loss on interruption
Design decision for crafter: Bash tool file ops vs Python migration module

steps.title: Proposal switch command and orchestrator routing
steps.description: Create new commands/proposal-switch.md that dispatches to sbir-orchestrator. Update orchestrator agent to handle switch: validate target exists (Glob .sbir/proposals/{id}/), update .sbir/active-proposal (Write tool), display target status summary.
acceptance_criteria: Switch updates .sbir/active-proposal to target proposal
Switch confirmation shows from/to proposals and target status
Nonexistent topic ID produces error listing available proposals
Switching to already-active proposal is idempotent with status display
Completed proposals switchable for Wave 9 debrief
architectural_constraints: New command markdown follows existing command pattern (see proposal-status.md)
Orchestrator validates target existence via Glob tool
wave-agent-mapping skill updated with switch routing

steps.title: Multi-proposal dashboard skill
steps.description: Create new skills/continue/multi-proposal-dashboard.md with enumeration patterns, display templates, corruption handling, and deadline sorting. This skill encodes the dashboard behavior that sbir-continue loads when multi-proposal layout is detected.
acceptance_criteria: Skill documents enumeration pattern: Glob .sbir/proposals/*/proposal-state.json
Skill provides table display template with topic ID, title, wave, deadline, status columns
Skill encodes active/completed separation logic
Skill encodes per-proposal corruption handling (error row, not crash)
Skill encodes deadline-driven suggestion pattern
architectural_constraints: Skill is domain knowledge, not executable code
Follows existing skill pattern (see continue-detection.md)

steps.title: Continue agent multi-proposal dashboard behavior
steps.description: Update sbir-continue agent behavioral spec and continue-detection skill. Add multi-proposal detection priority. When multi-proposal layout detected, load multi-proposal-dashboard skill and display table view. Single-proposal workspaces show familiar display.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows table of all proposals with key fields
Active and completed proposals visually separated
Closest deadline drives suggested action
Corrupted state for one proposal shows error row, not crash
Single-proposal workspace shows familiar non-table display
architectural_constraints: Read-only operation -- no state modifications
Per-proposal corruption caught and displayed per-row
Agent uses Glob + Read tools, not Python

steps.title: Completed proposal lifecycle and auto-switch
steps.description: Update orchestrator agent with auto-switch behavior after Wave 8 submission. Dashboard separation (active/completed) already handled by dashboard skill. Add selection prompt when multiple active remain.
acceptance_criteria: Auto-switch occurs when exactly one active proposal remains after submission
Selection prompt when multiple active proposals remain
All-completed workspace shows new-proposal or await-debrief guidance
architectural_constraints: Completion determined by wave 8 status, archived flag, or no-go decision
Auto-switch logic in orchestrator's submission confirmation flow

steps.title: Wave agent and skill path reference updates
steps.description: Batch update all 12 wave agents to reference orchestrator-provided resolved artifact base path instead of hardcoded artifacts/ references. Update proposal-state-patterns skill with multi-proposal path conventions.
acceptance_criteria: All agent markdown files reference resolved paths, not hardcoded .sbir/
proposal-state-patterns skill documents multi-proposal path conventions
Wave agents write artifacts to resolved artifact base path from dispatch context
architectural_constraints: Agents receive paths from orchestrator dispatch context
Batched update: all 12 wave agents in single step (identical pattern)
No Python changes -- these are markdown behavioral spec edits
steps.notes: This step batches all agent/skill path reference updates. They follow an identical substitution pattern.

python_modules_new: Path resolution module (~50-80 lines, adapter layer, PES hooks only)
python_modules_maybe: Migration module (~60-80 lines) -- may be agent-driven Bash ops instead

delivery_method_summary.code/tdd: 2 steps (01-01, 01-02) -- Python path resolution for PES

validation.batching_applied: Step 04-02 batches all 12 wave agent path reference updates (identical pattern)
validation.delivery_surface_separation: Python steps (01-01, 01-02) are clearly separated from markdown steps. No step mixes code/tdd with forge/agent-edit.
